bio.quote: Söldís Glóðfari is the master of the Glóðfari caravan — a well-worn group of six wagons that have been making circuits through the failing settlements of Svellheim for many seasons. Her coat is patchw

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

novice.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

system.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Vigilant, Handle Animals.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *Rapid Relocation*. Once per extended rest, you can reduce the travel time of a short leg of a journey for your group, provided conditions allow mounted or guided movement.

system.description: When you make a test using a skill from the exploration skill group and at least one of the d10s rolled is a 1, you can reroll one d10. You can use this perk only once per test.

novice.description: When you make a test using a skill from the exploration skill group and at least one of the d10s rolled is a 1, you can reroll one d10. You can use this perk only once per test.

system.description: The whole world’s a stage, and everyone on it, an actor. No one knows this better than the troubadour. You find energy in the drama of everyday life and know how to draw spectacle forth from even the most mundane of situations. You accent highs and deepen lows in service to whoever might witness your performance. As a troubadour, you chase drama. The insurmountable dangers of the world might cause many a hero to cower. But you take to that world stage not intending to die, but to find out if you are truly alive

But you take to that world stage not intending to die, but to find out if you are truly alive

system.description:
You can give a rousing speech, invoke your inspirations, or lift your fellows’ spirits, appealing to the muses to heighten a battle’s drama. However, irony is eager to hand your fortune to the villain to achieve the same end.

Before you roll to gain drama at the start of your turn, you can make your appeal (no action required). If you do, your roll gains the following additional effects:

  • If the roll is a 1, you { gain 1 additional drama}. The Director gains Malice.

  • If the roll is a 2, you , which you can keep or give to an ally within the distance of your active performance. The Director gains 1 Malice.

  • If the roll is a 3, you , which you can distribute among yourself and any allies within the distance of your active performance.

system.description:
During battles, you are fueled by the dynamic ups, downs, and upside downs of the fray, from which you derive a Heroic Resource called drama.

Drama in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain drama equal to your Milestones. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 drama. Additionally, you gain drama when certain events occur during a combat encounter:

  • The first time three or more heroes use an ability on the same turn, you gain 2 drama.

  • The first time any hero is made winded during the encounter, you gain 2 drama.

  • Whenever a creature within your line of effect rolls a natural 19 or 20, you gain 3 drama.

  • When you or another hero dies, you gain 10 drama.

When you are dead, you continue to gain drama during combat as long as your body is intact. If you have 30 drama during the encounter in which you died, you can come back to life with 1 Toughness and 0 drama (no action required). If you are still dead after the encounter in which you died, you can’t gain drama during future encounters.

You lose any remaining drama at the end of the encounter.

Drama Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain drama outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost drama without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs drama, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited drama on its effect, such as Artful Flourish, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of drama equal to your Milestones.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited drama on its effect, such as Artful Flourish, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of drama equal to your Milestones.

system.description:
You enter every battle with a set of performance abilities at the ready. Performances are magical presentations (such as songs, dances, poems, or gymnastic feats) that your allies can participate in. These abilities have the Persuasive keyword. At the start of each combat round, as long as you are not dazed, dead, or surprised, you can either choose a new performance or maintain your current performance (no action required). Your performance lasts until you are unable to maintain it or until the end of the encounter.

You start off with the Choreography and Revitalizing Limerick performance abilities. Your choice of class act grants you additional performances.

system.description:
Whenever you obtain a success on a test to interact with an NPC using a skill from the interpersonal group, you can form a bond with that NPC. When you enter into a negotiation with a bonded NPC, their patience increases by 1 (to a maximum of 5). Additionally, the first time during a negotiation that you personally make an argument that would increase a bonded NPC’s interest by 1, you instead increase their interest by 2 (to a maximum of 5).

You can have a number of bonds active equal to your level. When you form a bond with a new NPC that would exceed the limit, you must choose which of your active bonds to lose.

items.name: Allow Me to Introduce Tonightâ€™s Players

system.description:
Whenever you take the first turn in a combat encounter, you can use a main action to introduce yourself and your allies to your opponents.

Each ally can shift up to their speed, and ability rolls made against them have a double disadvantage (second chance) until the end of the combat round. Additionally,any surprised enemy is no longer surprised.

system.description: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

novice.description: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

system.description: You are a leading expert on a particular subject. Choose one skill you have from the lore skill group. You always have a double advantage (second chance) on tests made to recall lore using this skill. Additionally, your specialist knowledge grants you notoriety in fields related to the chosen skill. You treat your Renown as 1 higher when negotiating with an NPC who knows your reputation, or 2 higher if they have the same skill you chose for this perk.

system.description: You seek drama from story and recount, using your magic to manipulate the sequence of events unfolding before you. You have the Brag skill.

novice.description: You seek drama from story and recount, using your magic to manipulate the sequence of events unfolding before you. You have the Brag skill.
